reasoning: The stock recently dropped from $25.16 to $23.82 following weak revenue guidance and margin pressure announced on Feb 25, 2026. This creates a strong downward momentum and potential post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD). The price target from Wedbush was lowered to $23.00, potentially acting as a short-term anchor. On the positive side, the company announced an additional $350 million share repurchase program, which may provide some floor to the stock price. Considering the negative momentum but recognizing that short-term stock movements are noisy and slightly positive on average, I place the 1-week forecast slightly below 50% (0.47) and the 1-month forecast closer to 50% (0.49).
